protocol.name: xfce_output_private_v1
protocol.copyright:

interface.name: xfce_output_manager_private_v1
interface.description:
This protocol advertises extensions to the wlr_output_management_v1 protocol.
It is intended to be private to the Xfce desktop implementation, and should not be used by other applications. This protocol may introduce incompatible changes at any time, without warning, and without following normal, accepted guidelines about Wayland protocol versioning.
This protocol depends on the wlr_output_management_v1 protocol.
Clients that wish to use it MAY bind to both wlr_output_manager_v1 and xfce_output_manager_private_v1, but MUST then ignore any events on wlr_output_manager_v1. This allows for simplicity in the client implementation. For ease of use, the client should immediately release any wlr_output_head_v1 instances received from the wlr_output_manager_v1 global, and, if sharing the same wlr_output_head_v1 listener between the two manager instances, take care to avoid confusing head instances between the two managers.
Clients SHOULD still use the wlr_output_manager_v1 instance to create wlr_output_configuration_v1 objects.

request.name: stop
request.description:
Asks the manager to stop sending further events. The manager may continue to send events until sending the next done event, after which it will not send further events.
The client must not send any requests after this one.

event.name: head

description.summary: Notifies the client of a new head (second event)

event.description:
This provides the client with the xfce_output_private_v1 instance associated with an output.
The client can use the 'name' argument to correlate xfce_output_head_private_v1 and wlr_output_head_v1 instances.

event.name: done

description.summary: Sent all information about current configuration

event.description:
This indicates that the server has finished sending updates in the current atomic batch of changes.
The serial is used when calling wlr_output_manager_v1.create_configuration, and should be the same serial received with correlated updates from wlr_output_manager_v1.
See the documentation for wlr_output_manager_v1.done and wlr_output_configuration_v1.create_configuration for more information.

event.name: finished
event.description: This event is emitted when the manager intends to stop sending events permanently.

interface.name: xfce_output_head_private_v1

description.summary: An output head with extra information

interface.description: This describes a single output head, and is an extension interface along side wlr_output_head_v1.

event.name: edid

description.summary: Advertises the EDID of a device

event.description:
Emitted on creation of the xfce_output_head_private_v1 instance.
The EDID data is raw binary data exposed by the monitor's hardware and will need to be parsed in order to be useful.
Since the EDID will never change during the lifetime of this head object, it is safe to immediately send a release after receiving this event.

arg.name: fd
arg.summary: A file descriptor that can be mapped into memory

arg.name: size
arg.summary: The size of the memory region referred to by fd

event.name: finished
event.description: This event is emitted when the head is no longer available. Clients should stop using it and send the release request.

request.name: release
request.description: This request indicates to the compositor that the client will no longer use this head object.
